terms.definition: A tall plant with a thick, strong, woody trunk and many branches. Trees can grow very tall and live for many years.
terms.exampleUsage: The mango tree in our garden gives us shade and fruit.

terms.definition: A medium-sized plant with many thin, woody stems branching out near the ground. Shrubs are shorter than trees.
terms.exampleUsage: The rose shrub in our park has beautiful red flowers.

terms.definition: A small plant with a soft, green stem. Herbs usually live for only one or two seasons.
terms.exampleUsage: Mint is a herb that we use to make chutney.

terms.definition: A plant that grows upward by holding onto a support like a wall, fence, or stick.
terms.exampleUsage: The money plant is a climber that grows along the wall.

terms.definition: A plant with a weak stem that grows along the ground and spreads sideways.
terms.exampleUsage: The pumpkin is a creeper that spreads its vines on the ground.

terms.definition: A plant that is used to treat illnesses or keep us healthy. These plants have healing properties.
terms.exampleUsage: Tulsi is a medicinal plant used to cure cough and cold.

terms.definition: A gas that plants release into the air. All living things need oxygen to breathe and stay alive.
terms.exampleUsage: Plants give us oxygen which we need to breathe.

terms.definition: The washing away or blowing away of the top layer of soil. Plant roots help prevent soil erosion.
terms.exampleUsage: Planting more trees helps stop soil erosion near the river.

terms.definition: The thick, strong, main stem of a tree. The trunk supports the branches and leaves of the tree.
terms.exampleUsage: The trunk of the banyan tree is very thick and strong.

terms.definition: A soft material made from wood that is used to make paper. Trees are cut and their wood is turned into pulp.
terms.exampleUsage: Paper is made from wood pulp, so we should save paper to save trees.
